doctors.name: Jacopo Zabarella
doctors.biography: Italian Aristotelian philosopher and logician at Padua, whose treatises on method (regressus) shaped the methodological reflection of physicians trained in the Paduan school.
sources: Mikkeli, H. (1992). An Aristotelian Response to Renaissance Humanism.

doctors.name: Sanctorius Sanctorius
doctors.biography: Venetian physician, founder of quantitative iatromechanics; his Methodi vitandorum errorum (1603) is a sustained application of logical method to medicine.
sources: Bigotti, F. (2019). Physiology of the Soul.

doctors.name: Daniel Sennert
doctors.biography: Wittenberg physician and natural philosopher who deployed an Aristotelian-corpuscular logic in his medical works, mediating between scholastic logic and emerging chymistry.
sources: Newman, W. R. (2006). Atoms and Alchemy.
